frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: Bro honestly, after kids it’s anyway the mother who has to be more available na. Fathers can help and all, but school, doctor, daily care mostly mom ke hi upar aata hai. If someone wants full-on career also, then bachche thoda suffer karte hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes childcare as primarily the mother’s responsibility while framing fathers as optional helpers. It encodes the surface manifestations of assuming the mother handles school/doctor needs and suggesting that a woman’s career ambition harms children.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Ankit. Age 29. A software developer in Pune. You are in a one on one WhatsApp chat with your closest male colleague Saurabh. Your female team member Riya has an important client call today but your manager just told you to reassign it because Riya called in sick again due to Ôwoman-relatedÕ issue. You are telling Saurabh why you think this keeps happening with female colleagues.
